groups.title: Essentials
groups.description: Platform, format, offer, goal, audience, value prop, terms, URL, language, variants.

groups.title: Audience & Offer
groups.description: Awareness, need, outcome, differentiators, objections, proof, market.

groups.title: Brand & Creative
groups.description: Brand name, voice, tone, creative context, search/video/carousel inputs.

groups.title: Compliance & Output
groups.description: CTA, testing angles, regulated category, disclosures, prohibited claims.

variables.label: Ad platform
variables.placeholder: Select the advertising platform
variables.hint: Choose where the ads will be published so the correct asset structure and length rules can be applied.

help.why: The platform determines the required ad assets, formatting conventions, CTAs, and character handling.
help.example: Meta Ads
help.avoid: Mixing platform rules in one package without selecting a platform.

variables.label: Ad format
variables.placeholder: Choose a format or use Auto
variables.hint: Auto selects a suitable native format based on the platform and campaign goal.

help.why: Different formats require different copy elements, such as hooks, cards, headlines, or responsive assets.
help.example: Auto
help.avoid: Invented proof, fake urgency, personal-attribute claims, or guaranteed approval.

variables.label: Product or offer
variables.placeholder: Describe what you are advertising and what the customer receives.
variables.hint: Include the product, service, event, application, subscription, or lead offer being promoted.

help.why: The model cannot accurately describe or position an unspecified product or offer.
help.example: What is advertised and what the customer receives
help.avoid: Vague offers without a clear product or next step.

variables.label: Campaign goal
variables.placeholder: Select the main campaign goal
variables.hint: Choose the single most important action or outcome for this campaign.

help.why: The campaign goal determines the message hierarchy, conversion friction, and CTA.
help.example: Select the main campaign goal
help.avoid: Invented proof, fake urgency, personal-attribute claims, or guaranteed approval.

variables.label: Target audience
variables.placeholder: Who should see this ad, and what makes the offer relevant to them?
variables.hint: Describe the audience using useful business or interest context without including private personal data.

help.why: Audience relevance materially changes the language, angle, benefits, and level of explanation.
help.example: Who should see this ad, and what makes the offer relevant to them?
help.avoid: Invented proof, fake urgency, personal-attribute claims, or guaranteed approval.

variables.label: Core value proposition
variables.placeholder: What is the clearest reason someone should consider this offer?
variables.hint: Focus on the main value rather than a long list of features.

help.why: The central value proposition provides the primary persuasive idea for the campaign.
help.example: One clear reason to consider the offer
help.avoid: A long feature dump without a main idea.

variables.label: Offer details
variables.placeholder: Add verified prices, discounts, dates, bonuses, trial terms, or availability.
variables.hint: Only include terms that are current, accurate, and approved for advertising.

help.why: Real offer terms can materially strengthen conversion copy but must not be invented.
help.example: Add verified prices, discounts, dates, bonuses, trial terms, or availability.
help.avoid: Invented proof, fake urgency, personal-attribute claims, or guaranteed approval.

variables.label: Destination URL
variables.placeholder: https://example.com/landing-page
variables.hint: Enter the page users should reach after clicking the ad.

help.why: The destination helps identify the intended action and alerts the output to landing-page consistency requirements.
help.example: https://example.com/landing-page
help.avoid: Invented proof, fake urgency, personal-attribute claims, or guaranteed approval.

variables.label: Output language
variables.placeholder: English
variables.hint: Specify the language in which all generated copy should be written.

help.why: Language cannot always be inferred and can affect character use, tone, and platform limits.
help.example: English
help.avoid: Invented proof, fake urgency, personal-attribute claims, or guaranteed approval.

variables.label: Testing angles
variables.hint: Choose between 1 and 5 meaningfully different ad angles.

help.why: Paid advertising benefits from distinct testable concepts rather than one isolated piece of copy.
help.avoid: Invented proof, fake urgency, personal-attribute claims, or guaranteed approval.

variables.label: Audience awareness
variables.placeholder: Select how familiar the audience is
variables.hint: This controls how much context, education, and direct selling the ad should use.

help.why: Cold and warm audiences require materially different openings and conversion approaches.
help.example: Auto
help.avoid: Invented proof, fake urgency, personal-attribute claims, or guaranteed approval.

variables.label: Problem or need
variables.placeholder: What situation, frustration, task, or need makes the offer relevant?
variables.hint: Describe the need without using shame, fear, or unsupported assumptions about individuals.

help.why: A supplied need helps make the ad specific without forcing the model to invent audience pain points.
help.example: What situation, frustration, task, or need makes the offer relevant?
help.avoid: Invented proof, fake urgency, personal-attribute claims, or guaranteed approval.

variables.label: Desired outcome
variables.placeholder: What practical result is the audience hoping to achieve?
variables.hint: Use a realistic outcome that the product can genuinely support.

help.why: The desired outcome helps translate features into relevant benefits.
help.example: What practical result is the audience hoping to achieve?
help.avoid: Invented proof, fake urgency, personal-attribute claims, or guaranteed approval.

variables.label: Key differentiators
variables.placeholder: What makes this offer meaningfully different from common alternatives?
variables.hint: Include only distinctions that can be explained and substantiated.

help.why: Verified differentiation supports stronger positioning and prevents generic advertising.
help.example: What makes this offer meaningfully different from common alternatives?
help.avoid: Invented proof, fake urgency, personal-attribute claims, or guaranteed approval.

variables.label: Audience objections
variables.placeholder: What concerns might stop someone from clicking, signing up, or buying?
variables.hint: Examples may include price, complexity, trust, setup time, fit, or switching effort.

help.why: Real objections can produce useful test angles and more credible copy.
help.example: What concerns might stop someone from clicking, signing up, or buying?
help.avoid: Invented proof, fake urgency, personal-attribute claims, or guaranteed approval.

variables.label: Approved proof points
variables.placeholder: Add verified results, specifications, certifications, ratings, or evidence.
variables.hint: Only provide claims that may be used publicly and can be substantiated.

help.why: The model needs approved evidence before it can safely create proof-led advertising.
help.example: Only verified results with context
help.avoid: Invented testimonials, ratings, or ROI.

variables.label: Target market
variables.placeholder: For example: United States, Germany, EU, or worldwide
variables.hint: Add the country, region, or jurisdiction when it affects wording, currency, eligibility, or disclosures.

help.why: Advertising rules, offer availability, and market expectations can differ by jurisdiction.
help.example: For example: United States, Germany, EU, or worldwide
help.avoid: Invented proof, fake urgency, personal-attribute claims, or guaranteed approval.

variables.label: Brand name
variables.placeholder: Enter the public brand or business name
variables.hint: Use the name that should appear in the ad or advertising account.

help.why: Some platforms require a business name, while other ads may need clear advertiser identification.
help.example: Enter the public brand or business name
help.avoid: Invented proof, fake urgency, personal-attribute claims, or guaranteed approval.

variables.label: Brand voice
variables.placeholder: Select the closest brand voice
variables.hint: Choose the overall communication style rather than a temporary campaign emotion.

help.why: Brand voice changes wording and presentation while preserving the same factual message.
help.example: Clear and natural
help.avoid: Invented proof, fake urgency, personal-attribute claims, or guaranteed approval.

variables.label: Tone modifiers
variables.placeholder:
Conversational
Confident
Calm
Energetic
Empathetic
Concise
Educational
Time-sensitive but factual
variables.hint: Select only qualities that should noticeably influence this campaign. Select all that apply (one per line). Options: Conversational; Confident; Calm; Energetic; Empathetic; Concise; Educational; Time-sensitive but factual.

help.why: Tone modifiers allow campaign-level adjustment without duplicating the brand-voice field.
help.example: Add optional tone qualities
help.avoid: Invented proof, fake urgency, personal-attribute claims, or guaranteed approval.

variables.label: Creative context
variables.placeholder: Describe the image, video, product shot, demonstration, or existing creative.
variables.hint: The generated copy should complement the creative rather than contradict or repeat it.

help.why: Effective paid-ad copy must align with what the audience will actually see.
help.example: Describe the image, video, product shot, demonstration, or existing creative.
help.avoid: Invented proof, fake urgency, personal-attribute claims, or guaranteed approval.

variables.label: Search keywords
variables.placeholder: List priority searches or keyword themes, separated by commas.
variables.hint: Use verified keyword targets from the relevant search campaign.

help.why: Search-ad headlines and descriptions should reflect the intent of the supplied keyword set.
help.example: List priority searches or keyword themes, separated by commas.
help.avoid: Invented proof, fake urgency, personal-attribute claims, or guaranteed approval.

variables.label: Video opening
variables.placeholder: What happens or appears during the first few seconds?
variables.hint: Describe the opening shot, action, speaker, demonstration, or available footage.

help.why: A video hook should match the real opening visual rather than describe footage that does not exist.
help.example: What happens or appears during the first few seconds?
help.avoid: Invented proof, fake urgency, personal-attribute claims, or guaranteed approval.

variables.label: Carousel cards
variables.hint: Choose between 2 and 10 cards.

help.why: The number of cards directly determines the message progression and amount of card copy.
help.avoid: Invented proof, fake urgency, personal-attribute claims, or guaranteed approval.

variables.label: Preferred CTA
variables.placeholder: Select a CTA or use Auto
variables.hint: Auto chooses a standard platform CTA that matches the campaign goal.

help.why: The preferred action affects both button selection and the closing language of the ad.
help.example: Auto
help.avoid: Invented proof, fake urgency, personal-attribute claims, or guaranteed approval.

variables.label: Preferred ad angles
variables.placeholder:
Auto
Core benefit
Problem and solution
Product use case
Differentiation
Approved proof
Offer or promotion
Objection handling
variables.hint: Only select angles that match the available facts and campaign strategy. Select all that apply (one per line). Options: Auto; Core benefit; Problem and solution; Product use case; Differentiation; Approved proof; Offer or promotion; Objection handling.

help.why: Advertisers may need specific strategic angles while the model still prevents unsupported use of proof or offers.
help.example: Auto
help.avoid: Invented proof, fake urgency, personal-attribute claims, or guaranteed approval.

variables.label: Regulated category
variables.placeholder: Select a category when relevant
variables.hint: This activates more cautious claim handling and additional review notes.

help.why: High-stakes and regulated advertising requires materially different wording and review safeguards.
help.example: None
help.avoid: Health/finance claims without approved disclosures.

variables.label: Required disclosures
variables.placeholder: Paste approved legal text, eligibility terms, warnings, or required qualifiers.
variables.hint: Use exact approved wording where possible and state where it must appear.

help.why: The model cannot safely invent legally required disclosures or jurisdiction-specific wording.
help.example: Paste approved legal text, eligibility terms, warnings, or required qualifiers.
help.avoid: Invented proof, fake urgency, personal-attribute claims, or guaranteed approval.

variables.label: Claims to avoid
variables.placeholder: List prohibited promises, phrases, comparisons, or product claims.
variables.hint: Include internal brand restrictions and wording that must not appear.

help.why: Advertiser-specific restrictions cannot always be inferred from general policy rules.
help.example: List prohibited promises, phrases, comparisons, or product claims.
help.avoid: Invented proof, fake urgency, personal-attribute claims, or guaranteed approval.

variables.label: Additional requirements
variables.placeholder: Add any remaining instructions that materially affect the final ad package.
variables.hint: Use this for essential requirements not covered by the other fields.

help.why: One controlled catch-all field prevents multiple overlapping miscellaneous inputs.
help.example: Add any remaining instructions that materially affect the final ad package.
help.avoid: Invented proof, fake urgency, personal-attribute claims, or guaranteed approval.
